insights:
The single best deal is DeepSeek V4 Flash, which tops the composite value score at 666.7. It delivers an intelligence score of 40 out of 60, a blended price of just $0.06 per million tokens, a speed of 104 tokens per second, and a 1,000K context window. For cost-sensitive production use, this model offers the highest intelligence per dollar among all options, making it the default recommendation for developers who want capable reasoning without paying a premium.

Two strong runners-up merit consideration. MiMo-V2.5 offers nearly identical pricing at $0.06/1M and a similar 1,000K context, but its intelligence score of 37 is slightly lower and its speed is only 64 t/s, making it better suited for latency-tolerant tasks where the extra context is critical. For higher intelligence needs, DeepSeek V4 Pro scores 44/60 at $0.18/1M — a threefold cost increase that yields a 10% intelligence gain over the Flash variant. Inclusion AI’s Ling-2.6-flash, at the same $0.06 price, is exceptionally fast at 179 t/s but scores only 14/60, so it is viable only for trivial classification or simple extraction where speed outweighs quality.

For the most common cost-sensitive API use case — high-volume, multi-turn chat or general-purpose reasoning — the actionable recommendation is to default to DeepSeek V4 Flash. Its combination of 40/60 intelligence, $0.06 pricing, 104 t/s throughput, and 1M context provides the best balance of capability and economy. If your application demands higher accuracy on complex tasks and the budget allows, upgrade to DeepSeek V4 Pro at $0.18/1M. For purely cost-optimised, low-cognition pipelines, consider Ling-2.6-flash to minimise latency.
